summary: Matched Jigglypuff/Purin SpecialN functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.c, replacing several placeholder function comments with C bodies and making smaller matching-oriented edits to nearby anim, phys, IASA, and collision code. Bot evidence reports 9 new function matches and one .sdata2 unmatched-item regression.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; gained matched implementations and helper inlines for Purin SpecialN animation, physics, IASA, and collision behavior.

what_changed.text: Replaced the local NEGABS macro with SIGNF and switched absolute-value checks and assignments to the existing ABS macro in several release/turn physics and input paths.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPr_SpecialNRelease_IASA uses ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) and SIGNF(fp->input.lstick.x)
raw_diff: ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys use ABS

what_changed.text: Added static inline helpers for recurring or matching-sensitive operations: ftPr_JObjSetRotationY, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim_inline, setFacingDir, wallBounceEffect, and ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll_inline.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added static inline void ftPr_JObjSetRotationY
raw_diff: added static inline void ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim_inline
raw_diff: added static inline void setFacingDir
raw_diff: added static inline void wallBounceEffect
raw_diff: added static inline void ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll_inline

what_changed.text: Implemented ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim, including scale interpolation from ftPr_Init_803D05C8, hit-capsule handling, roll angle normalization, angle-crossing checks, and top-joint rotation handling.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim +1380 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Implemented ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll, including wall-hit detection from env_flags, wall bounce visual/audio/camera effects, velocity and charge damping, motion-state transition to aerial charge release on loss of ground collision, and callback/rotation cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added wallBounceEffect and calls to efSync_Spawn, Camera_80030E44, ftCommon_8007EBAC, ft_PlaySFX
text_corpus: bot report lists ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll +928 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Implemented ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll, including wall-bounce handling, ground collision landing transition, velocity transfer from aerial to grounded state, landing effect spawn, input-driven direction update, and cliff-catch cleanup.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll +1496 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Made smaller matching edits to already-partial functions including ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_IASA, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys, and ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists these functions moving to 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Factored duplicated facing-direction reset/update logic into setFacingDir rather than keeping repeated nested blocks in ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys replaces duplicated facing update blocks with setFacingDir(fp)

smart_moves.text: Factored wall bounce effect setup into wallBounceEffect and reused it in both grounded and aerial release collision implementations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll and ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll call wallBounceEffect for positive and negative directions

smart_moves.text: Used the project ABS macro for absolute-value expressions instead of preserving a file-local NEGABS helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: NEGABS removal and ABS call sites
decomp_standards: canonical-control-flow-and-macros recommends project macros such as ABS when they express the operation

smart_moves.text: Kept some matching-sensitive stack shaping narrow and explicit with PAD_STACK or small padding arrays at local sites rather than broad pragmas or assembly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8 __[8] in ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys and u8 _pad[4] in ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter motion code, small helper inlines can express repeated local behavior and improve matchability without resorting to broad codegen controls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: setFacingDir helper reused in ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys
raw_diff: wallBounceEffect helper reused in grounded and aerial collision functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a local absolute-value macro appears generated or redundant, checking for and using the project ABS macro can simplify matching source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: NEGABS removed; ABS used for stick input, velocities, slopes, ECB extents, and vertical velocity checks

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching may require declaration-order and local-lifetime adjustments, including assigning a pointer after another declaration or hoisting temporaries before use.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hitCapsuleToggle changes ftPurinAttributes* da declaration and assignment order
raw_diff: ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim hoists f32 angle and uses PAD_STACK(8)
raw_diff: max_vel and min_vel assigned inside ABS comparisons

naming_conventions.name: SIGNF
naming_conventions.lesson: A local sign helper was introduced for repeated conversion of a float to +/-1.0f direction.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: SIGNF used in ftPr_SpecialNRelease_IASA and ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll

naming_conventions.name: setFacingDir
naming_conventions.lesson: Helper name directly describes the local operation of applying pending specialn.facing_dir to fighter direction fields and resetting state.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline void setFacingDir(Fighter* fp)

naming_conventions.name: wallBounceEffect
naming_conventions.lesson: Helper name reflects combined wall-bounce effect side effects: spawn effect, camera event, common effect call, and SFX.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: helper calls efSync_Spawn, Camera_80030E44, ftCommon_8007EBAC, ft_PlaySFX

naming_conventions.name: ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim_inline and ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll_inline
naming_conventions.lesson: File-local inline helpers use the owning function name plus _inline when they appear to represent extracted code needed by one nearby matched function.

naming_conventions.name: mf, mf1, mf2
naming_conventions.lesson: The file retains short motion-flag names and comments them with TODOs where flags are not yet semantically named.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: /// @todo Named flags. static u32 const mf = ...
raw_diff: existing ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys context retains mf1 and mf2 use

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK and small local padding arrays for narrow stack-layout shaping.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence includes bot match results but does not explain each padding site's necessity; do not generalize beyond this file without objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline a JObj rotation setter with explicit jobj.h assert expansions and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty call.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPr_JObjSetRotationY contains __assert("jobj.h", 660, "jobj") and __assert("jobj.h", 661, ...), writes jobj->rotate.y, checks JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This conflicts with the general preference to map header assert expansions back to existing helpers when possible; PR evidence only shows it was used in this match, not that it should be a global style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use assignment inside ABS comparison to shape max/min velocity clamp code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Evidence supports this as a local matching shape in ftPr_SpecialN.c only.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Factor repeated collision side effects into an inline helper while preserving caller-local temporaries for stack and call-shape needs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: wallBounceEffect takes Vec3* pos and f32* angle; callers declare local Vec3 pos and f32 angle before invoking it
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No reviewer explanation is present; inferred from diff structure and reported matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The author reports iterative use of Claude and the permuter to reach the match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Took a lot of iteration with Claude, and some help from the permuter, but we got there in the end!
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is author process evidence only; it does not validate any generated source shape by itself.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code increased to 59.65%, +0.19%, +7480 bytes, with 9 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.

New 100% matches reported: ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim, ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys, ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys, and ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim.
Bot reported 1 regression in an unmatched item: .sdata2 in the same unit moved from 98.81% to 96.51%, -2 bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: 📉 1 regression in an unmatched item table

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or review records were included in the slice.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merge state, changed-file counts
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for the single changed file
notes: The diff and bot report strongly support changed files, matched functions, and local matching tactics.
No human review comments were available, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale cannot be inferred beyond merge metadata and bot output.
The author mentions Claude and permuter use, but the slice does not identify which specific code shapes came from those tools.
Padding, declaration-order, and custom inline choices are visible in the diff, but per-site objdiff rationale is not included.
The bot report includes a .sdata2 regression; the slice does not explain whether maintainers considered it acceptable.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftPurin SpecialN matching used helpers plus ABS cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2347 matched several ftPr_SpecialN functions by replacing placeholders with C bodies, using local static inline helpers for repeated behavior, replacing a local NEGABS macro with ABS plus SIGNF, and applying narrow stack/local-shape tactics. Bot reported 9 new matches in the unit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Caller-local temporaries can pair with inline helpers for collision effect matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: wallBounceEffect centralizes wall-bounce effect side effects, while callers still declare local Vec3 and angle temporaries before invoking it. This shape appears in both grounded and aerial SpecialN release collision functions that the bot reported as newly matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: wallBounceEffect and caller-local Vec3 pos/f32 angle

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution for custom inlines that preserve expanded header asserts
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2347 used a file-local ftPr_JObjSetRotationY inline containing explicit jobj.h assert expansions to match ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim. This is useful historical evidence that a match may sometimes preserve an expanded assert shape, but it should remain an exception requiring objdiff evidence because current standards prefer existing header helpers when identifiable.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential nuance for the header-inlines standard; route to curator because one PR does not establish a global exception.

source_update_candidates.title: Emphasize that PAD_STACK and local padding need per-site verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2347 contains PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), and small u8 padding arrays in a successful matching PR, but the slice does not explain each site's necessity. This reinforces the existing standard that stack-shaping tactics should be narrow and backed by objdiff evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate supporting example for an existing standard, not a new accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: ftPr_SpecialN.c includes Purin SpecialN animation, physics, IASA, and collision functions
source_update_candidates.text: The changed file src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.c contains Purin SpecialN functions including release/turn animation, release/turn physics, release IASA, and grounded/aerial release collision handlers.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path-classification update for curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that Claude-generated code or permuter output is generally acceptable style; the PR body only says these tools helped during iteration.
Do not promote the custom ftPr_JObjSetRotationY assert-expanded inline as a general replacement for HSD_JObjSet* helpers; current standards prefer header helpers when identifiable.
Do not treat the .sdata2 regression as approved data-section practice; the slice reports the regression but provides no rationale.
Do not claim every padding site is necessary without per-site objdiff or author explanation.
